meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Student Guide: Using Analytics to Build a Pro Portfolio
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with no portfolio. Learn how to implement analytics in your first AI app to prove user value to employers. A step-by-step beginner guide.
content.answer_block: To solve the 'no portfolio' problem, you must prove your apps provide real value. By integrating analytics, you transform a simple project into a data-backed case study. This guide shows you how to track user interactions using Antigravity and Claude Code, giving you metrics to showcase your technical impact.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks grinding through tutorials, but your GitHub remains a graveyard of 'To-Do' lists and generic clones. The silence from recruiters isn't because you lack skill; it's because you lack evidence. In a crowded market, your code is just text—unless you can prove that people actually use it.

Today, we stop building 'projects' and start building 'products.' By embedding analytics into your workflow, you move from being a student who writes code to an engineer who understands user behavior. This is the bridge between a classroom assignment and a professional portfolio that demands attention.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Identify the one action that proves your app is useful. For a builder, this isn't just 'page views'—it's 'successful AI query' or 'user retention'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project codebase and suggest the three most critical user interaction events to track for a high-impact portfolio case study.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze the business logic and identify key success events for analytics'

steps.title: Initialize Analytics Middleware
steps.description: Don't clutter your main logic. Create a dedicated analytics service that wraps your API calls to ensure clean, maintainable code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular analytics middleware service that can be plugged into my existing API routes with minimal boilerplate.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Create analytics-service.ts with TypeScript interfaces'

steps.title: Instrument the AI Interaction
steps.description: Capture the prompt and the response metadata. This demonstrates to employers that you know how to track LLM performance and user satisfaction.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a function to track the latency and token usage of my AI response, ensuring the data is sent to my analytics endpoint asynchronously.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Instrument the AI chat route to track latency and token count'

steps.title: Build the Performance Dashboard
steps.description: Create a simple admin view that visualizes your user data. Seeing your own 'active user' count is the ultimate motivation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a simple React component that fetches the last 24 hours of event logs and displays them in a clean, professional dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a dashboard component to visualize analytics data'

steps.title: Documenting Your Impact
steps.description: Translate your analytics into a 'Case Study' file. Use your data to write a README that explains the 'why' behind your design choices.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a README.md section titled 'Impact Analysis' that explains how these analytics prove the app's efficiency and user engagement.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create a README section about the analytics-driven impact of the project'

pro_tips: Track 'failed' events as aggressively as successful ones; showing you debugged errors based on data is a senior-level trait.
Never send PII (Personally Identifiable Information) to your analytics; always hash user IDs to maintain ethical standards.
Use latency tracking to show potential employers you care about the end-user experience, not just the code performance.
Keep your analytics payload under 1KB to ensure your app stays lightning-fast for users.
Connect your analytics to a public-facing 'stats' page to make your portfolio project feel alive and production-ready.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to Vercel or Netlify, share the link in your LinkedIn bio, and start tracking your first real users to populate your new dashboard.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student following a guide—you are an AI engineer with a data-backed product. This shift in perspective is exactly what separates the top 1% of candidates from the rest. Stop waiting for permission to build. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
